South Africa · Wild Coast · 2025

Edge of the
Wild Coast

Five days on the Pondo Trail

RoutePort Edward → Manteku
Distance~70 kilometres
Group24 adventurers
Scroll
The Journey

Five days on the
edge of the world

After eighteen months of plans, messages, and anticipation — the Pondo Trail begins. Five days of coastal trails, river crossings, and shared stories along one of South Africa's last truly wild coastlines.

From Port Edward to Manteku, the trail threads past shipwrecks and champagne waterfalls, along cliffs that drop sheer into the Indian Ocean.

1
Port Edward → MsikabaOcean safari · Mtentu landing · 15 km
2
Msikaba → LuphutanaGrosvenor shipwreck · Champagne Mile · 19 km
3
Luphutana → MbotyiWaterfall Bluff · Cathedral Rock · 20 km
4
Mbotyi → MantekuPrivate estuary · Pondo hospitality · 16 km
"The trail threads through country that feels untouched — as if the continent ends here and the ocean has always had the final word."
Msikaba Gorge · Day 1
Day One
Day 1 · Port Edward to Msikaba

Gathering at the
Edge of the Wild Coast

Eighteen months of plans, messages, and anticipation lead to this moment

At last, the Pondo Trail begins — after 18 months of planning — united by excitement and a hint of nerves. The air hums with the unknown that lies ahead: five days of coastal trails, river crossings, and shared stories. Morning light spills across Port Edward as laughter rises and backpacks are adjusted.

The first day unfolds across roughly 15 kilometres. It begins with an ocean safari skimming over soft morning swells, dolphins keeping pace and whales rising nearby before we land near Mtentu. From there, the trail threads through Mkambathi's open grasslands.

The final stretch leads to Msikaba, where the estuary glows in the fading light and ice-cold glasses of gin pass between outstretched hands — the first of many shared moments to come.

"Day 2 starts before sunrise — a calm paddle up the Msikaba estuary, then 19 kilometres of the Wild Coast at its most dramatic."
Day 2 · Msikaba to Luphutana
"Where a waterfall spills straight into the ocean — and the Wild Coast truly reveals itself."
Day 3 · Waterfall Bluff
Day Two
Day 2 · Msikaba to Luphutana

Shipwrecks &
Champagne Showers

19 kilometres of coastline, swim spots, and the trail's most loved ritual

Day 2 starts before sunrise with a calm paddle up the Msikaba estuary, followed by breakfast back at camp before the real test begins: 19 km of coastline, swim spots, and long sandy stretches that pushed everyone a little.

The trail passes the Grosvenor shipwreck before opening onto Lambazi Beach. Day 2 officially birthed the motto: "If Steve can…"

The afternoon rolls into Champagne Mile and the legendary Pondo Shower — a cold, brilliant blast marking the final steps of the day. Spirits lift, legs wobble, and laughter carries the group into Luphutana Pondo Camp.

Day 3 — Waterfall Wonderland · Luphutana to Mbotyi · 20 km

Day three is when the Wild Coast truly reveals itself — sheer cliffs dropping into churning surf, quiet green valleys, and viewpoints that halt the group mid-stride.

Day 3 · Luphutana to Mbotyi
Day Three
Day 3 · Luphutana to Mbotyi

Waterfall
Wonderland

Majestic cliffs, hidden valleys, and the roar of the ocean

Day three is when the Wild Coast truly reveals itself. The trail stretches roughly 20 kilometres, unfolding landmark after landmark — sheer cliffs dropping into churning surf, quiet green valleys, and viewpoints that halt the group mid-stride.

We trace the edge of the continent to Waterfall Bluff, where a waterfall spills straight into the ocean, before moving on to Cathedral Rock and the tucked-away paths of Cutwini Valley.

Lunch arrives Pondo-style: boerewors and crayfish over the coals, washed down with a cold beer. Tired legs, salty smiles, and the thrill of an unforgettable day settle in at Mbotyi River Lodge.

Day Four · The Finish
Manteku · Day 4 · The Finish

Where the trail
ends in pure joy

Some melt into massages, others wade into the lagoon, and the boys start tossing a ball like it's a championship final. Everywhere you look: the unmistakable glow of we made it.
